config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, mayoritas bisa semena-mena terhadap yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, bukan ketika mereka dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual; in the United States, I am much more comfortable with a system that treats individual rights as the default starting point. That said, I do not believe rights are absolute in every case, because public safety, disease control, and preventing harm can justify limits. The problem with the statement is that it is too broad and too easy to use as an excuse for majorities or governments to trample unpopular people. A healthy society should protect the common good by respecting individual rights, not by treating those rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk menindas hak dasar individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan dari mayoritas, padahal yang paling rentan justru kelompok kecil atau tidak populer. Memang ada situasi tertentu, seperti bencana atau wabah, di mana pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan tidak menghapus martabat orang. Jadi bagi saya, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang melindungi individu sekaligus memungkinkan kepentingan umum berjalan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement is too broad for me, and I do not accept it as written. From an American perspective, individual rights should be the default priority because history shows how quickly “society’s interests” can become a cover for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular people. That said, I do not believe rights are unlimited; public health, emergency response, and preventing direct harm can justify narrow restrictions if they are truly necessary and temporary. In my view, a strong society is one that earns the common good by protecting individual liberty, not one that asks people to surrender their rights first and hope for fairness later.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia justru sering terlihat bahwa dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, bukan untuk benar-benar menyejahterakan כולם. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tapi harus menjadi batas utama supaya mayoritas, negara, atau kelompok tertentu tidak semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi, menurut saya yang benar bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tetap menempatkan martabat dan hak dasar manusia sebagai fondasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States I think it is far too easy for “society’s interests” to become a slogan for censorship, discrimination, or plain government overreach. My default position is that individual rights come first, and any restriction has to be narrowly justified by real harm, not vague appeals to the common good. I do take public health and emergency situations seriously, but those limits should be temporary, proportional, and subject to strong accountability. A free society should protect the common good by defending rights, not by treating them as something the majority can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
